title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 20 August 2024.

items.title: **Dead Cells** receives its final major update today, seven years after release
bullets: "The End is Near" is the 35th update to the game
The original designer of Dead Cells, Sébastien Benard, formed a new studio and has a new roguelike game on the way called Tenjutsu in which players take the role of a renegade yakuza.
Earlier this year, Benard called the decision to end Dead Cells development "the worst imaginable asshole move".

items.title: **Final Fantasy 16** gets a September release date for PC
bullets: Free demo available to try now
Originally released for PS5 in June 2023, it will make the jump to PC on September 17, 2024, with the base game priced at $49.99 / £44.99 via Steam and the Epic Games Store.
Final Fantasy 16 Complete Edition, which includes the base game plus its story expansions Echoes of the Fallen and The Rising Tide, is priced at $69.99 / £64.99.

items.title: **Funko Fusion** gets a PC demo and Mega Man reveal trailer
bullets: The free PC demo includes the first level of Funko Fusion’s world inspired by 1982 horror classic The Thing.
The game will be released on September 13 for PS5, Xbox Series S/X and PC, and on November 15 for PS4 and Nintendo Switch.

items.title: **Gamescom** Opening Night Live today
bullets: 2 p.m. EDT / 11 a.m. PDT / 7 p.m. BST / 8 p.m. CEST

items.title: **Climax** opens new studio in Scotland
bullets: Climax Studios has opened a new location in Edinburgh.
The studio opened its doors in February, and has been recruiting a team over the past few months.
